rent.note: furnished 1BR in Chania centre; €450–750/mo; €600–900 with views

utilities.note: electric + water + internet; summer A/C adds cost

diningOut.note: tavernas and cafes; tourist areas pricier

transit.note: local buses; some walking; scooter rental common

coworking.note: small coworking market; budget assumes flexible desk/cafe mix rather than Athens-scale supply

misc.note: gym, toiletries, entertainment, beach and day-trip buffer

accomSeasonality._doc: 12 monthly multipliers for RENT ONLY (Jan..Dec). 1.0 = base.

evidence.quote: For rent in the Chania center, you can expect €600–€900 per month for a one-bedroom apartment
A comfortable monthly budget in Crete is €1,000–€1,600, which includes rent, groceries, transport, utilities, dining out, and entertainment

evidence.claim: Chania has strong fixed internet for a smaller island city, but coworking supply is limited
evidence.quote: Chania has major digital infrastructure including world-class fiber connectivity with speeds of 100 Mbps to 200 Mbps in urban centers

evidence.claim: Greek prepaid mobile data plans support the €15–20/mo SIM assumption
evidence.quote: prepaid data bundles are commonly available from COSMOTE, Vodafone, and Nova
